tr.td: Cholesterol in LDL [Mass/volume] in Serum or Plasma by Direct assay
Cholesterol in HDL [Mass/volume] in Serum or Plasma

tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows elevated HbA1c, indicating impaired glucose control or possible diabetes, and significantly high total and LDL cholesterol, suggesting increased cardiovascular risk. Carbon dioxide is mildly decreased, which may reflect mild metabolic acidosis or respiratory alkalosis. Other parameters, including kidney function, electrolytes, and triglycerides, are within normal limits. Recommend further evaluation and management of glycemic control and dyslipidemia.
Cholesterol in LDL [Mass/volume] in Serum or Plasma by Direct assay
